CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-26-2023 Set For Order To Show Cause On Aug-01-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT (CONT. FROM 5/23/23)
Off Calendar May-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Alexander Morrow, Esq. 415-421-6500 Shartsis Friese LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is not contested.The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant AirBnB's demurrer to the fourth amended complaint-his fifth attempt to plead- is sustained without leave to amend.

Plaintiff argues that Defendant collected profits from the unauthorized rental of his home by third parties also named in this action.

Plaintiff first asserts a claim for conversion, alleging that Defendant failed to comply with an Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court order to the place the rental income of his home into an interest bearing account and that it acted as an agent of the other defendants to wrongfully exercise control of Plaintiff's property. (4AC, 6:27-28, 7:1; Ex. A). Plaintiff fails to allege that Defendant's interference was "knowing or intentional." (See CACI 2100.) Here, the purported order from the court was quite vague, it did not list the address or identify the property at issue and Defendant was not a party to the order. By Plaintiff's own admission, Defendant asked him for an updated order from the court and he did not provide one. (4AC, 4:22-23.) Plaintiff fails to state facts sufficient to demonstrate conversion. Additionally, Plaintiff fails to show that Defendant is an agent of the other named defendants. Allowing leave to amend cannot cure these defects. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Plaintiff also a claim for property obtained under false pretenses, arguing that Defendant is liable for the acts of the other named defendants, for the receipt of monies by the use of a false pretense. (4AC, 9:8-9.) Plaintiff fails to state facts sufficient to demonstrate false pretenses, as he cannot show that Defendant had any intent to defraud him. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Airbnb's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. A declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Airbnb's response to the fourth amended complaint is now due by April 13, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-05-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.
Under Section 230(c)(1) of the CDA, "No provider or user of an interactive computer service shall be treated as the publisher or speaker of any information provided by another information content provider." 47 U.S.C. Sec. 230(c)(1). Section 230's preemption provision further states that "[n]o cause of action may be brought and no liability may be imposed under any State or local law that is inconsistent with this section." 47 U.S.C. Sec. 230(e)(3). For the CDA to shield a party from liability, the party must be "(1) a provider or user of an interactive computer service (2) whom a plaintiff seeks to treat, under a state law cause of action, as a publisher or speaker (3) of information provided by another information content provider." Barnes v. Yahoo!, Inc., 570 F.3d 1096, 1100-01 (9th Cir. 2009).
Notably, "section 230(c)(1) precludes liability that treats a website as the publisher or speaker of information users provide on the website." Doe v. Internet Brands, Inc., 824 F.3d 846, 850 (9th Cir. 2016). "This grant of immunity applies only if the interactive computer service provider is not also an 'information content provider,' which is defined as someone who is 'responsible, in whole or in part, for the creation or development of' the offending content." Fair Hous. Council of San Fernando Valley v. Roommates.Com, LLC, 521 F.3d 1157, 1162 (9th Cir. 2008) (en banc).
Here, 1) Defendant is a provider of an interactive computer service, 2) Plaintiff brings brings state law causes of action against Defendant but avoids directly stating that Defendant is a "publisher" or "speaker" 3) of information provided by provided by another information content provider (Defendants Gori and Sendas Ribeiro).
"[T]he correct test under section 230 is not whether a challenged activity merely bears some connection to online content but whether the claim inherently requires the court to treat the interactive computer service as a publisher or speaker of information provided by another." (La Park La Brea A LLC v. Airbnb, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 285 F.Supp.3d 1097, 1108.
Plaintiff brings a similar argument to that in La Park La Brea A, where he, albeit indirectly, argues that Defendant is an information content provider that contributes to the alleged illegality of the conduct-by allowing Gori and Sendas Ribeiro to illegally list on its cite and collect the rental profits-and thus falls outside of the CDA's grant of immunity (TAC, 4-6) (calling the CDA claim "frivolous.") (Opp., 11:20-21.)
However, courts have granted CDA protection to websites that process payments and transactions in connection with third-party listings, including Airbnb. (La Park at 1106.) Thus, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege he is suing over the court-order violation only and to remove the claims regarding the website posting and the processing of payments (TAC 5-9) which are preempted by the CDA.

Order signed.

Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/VP)
